How important is skilling up the cards in this game? Is that why my shields are always like... 20% of incoming damage?
lv1 skill is half as effective as lv6 skills
so if u max skill all u still shield like 40% of dmg
So 100% increased effect on the way to 6?

like if ur defense is 10 shield rn at lv1
lv6 def would be 20 shield
If lv1 is half of 6, then 6 is also double of 1.

Pretty important, depending on the cards they have different effects.

Some gives you more alienus for example

While other deal damages

Or gives more shield

Or their effects get more pronounced, stuff like dmg reduction for example
ye typically its 2x from lv1 to lv6
In this case, my ~~Lily's~~ Faint's 3 cost only gives 17 shield. But enemies are hitting for 60-100. Lol
If i remember correctly the 3 cost was also giving you more stacks of endure
Wups my bad, I mean Faint. Shes also in my team.
ah yeah shes not a good shielder
Shes a good deaths door-er though. XD
yeah shes mainly for death resistance
Faint 3 cost is kind of useless
It is? Well shoot.... but doesnt it allow me to death resist?
It's more heal if you trigger death res
The only death res stats is from your exalt
You use her as death res bot
Understood, so thats where my 200% death res was coming from.

Pandia has a built-in 35% final damage modifier against multiattacks and a *relatively* efficient way of converting damage into counter, which also has prepare on it

Basically you use her rouse and 3 cost. Then a couple of reloads . Reloads make it so that when an ally uses their exalt Vort does an extra attack called pursuit (like follow up attack in HSR if you're familiar with that). After 3 pursuits you change tentacle stance to red and press Vort exalt and boom
